Cast: Patrick Page (Performer)
Trading Notes:
From the STC website: Macbeth. Iago. Claudius. STC Affiliated Artist Patrick Page invites you to experience the evolution of evil in Shakespeare’s villains—from rogues and cutthroats to tyrants and sociopaths. Patrick Page explores how Shakespeare created the treacherous characters we all love to hate. This mesmerizing one-man performance is available online only.
The exact recording date is unclear, but it was available to view online starting on February 4, 2021.

Master Notes: Great capture of this immensely hyped production! Shot in a great mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups; the far right side of the stage is often cut off by a bar or lighting fixtures, but it's worked around well and not much action is missed because of this. Minimal wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout. Audio is patched in from an external source. Includes bows. Slightly Zegler-focused; can you blame me? Overall a great capture of this inevitably Broadway-bound summer blockbuster.

Cast: Reeve Carney (Orpheus), Eva Noblezada (Eurydice), Amber Gray (Persephone), Patrick Page (Hades), André De Shields (Hermes), Jewelle Blackman (Fate), Yvette González-Nacer (Fate), Kay Trinidad (Fate), Afra Hines (Worker), Timothy Hughes (Worker), John Krause (Worker), Kimberly Marable (Worker), Ahmad Simmons (Worker)
Trading Notes:
Previews! Picks up after Road to Hell. Decent capture with a lot of beautiful zooms, two really annoying heads, and a drop out occasionally but never for long. (For the first time in my mastering career I kept putting the camera down after musical numbers because I couldn't NOT clap). This was my first time seeing the show and hearing this music so I was sometimes focused on the wrong things. Oh well. Overall, I love this video. I got home at 2AM after filming it and just SOBBED while I rewatched some of my favorite moments. WHAT A SHOW!

Cast: Madeline Powell (Eliza Doolittle), Jonathan Grunert (Henry Higgins), Cameron Loyal (Freddy Eynsford-Hill), Becky Saunders (Mrs. Higgins), Michael Hegarty (Alfred P. Doolittle), Madeline Brennan (Mrs. Pearce), Torinae (Mrs. Eynsford-Hill), Blair Beasley (Mrs. Eynsford-Hill), John Adkison (Colonel Hugh Pickering), Daniel James Canaday (Professor Zoltan Karpathy), Diana Craig (Queen of Transylvania), Richard Coleman (Jamie), Kevin D. O'Neil (Harry), Maeghin Mueller (Mrs. Hopkins)
Trading Notes: Excellent 4K capture of this newly launched non-equity tour during its first month on the road. It's filmed in 16:9 from the center mezzanine, with a mix of wides, mediums, and closeups. There's no major obstruction or blackouts other than a quick dropout during I've Grown Accustomed to Her Face and a couple of heads that can be seen at the bottom of the screen during wides, that only cover actors' legs, mostly during Act One. Due to the nature of the show's lighting, spotlights sometimes affect faces and the picture's not as sharp during some of the darker scenes, but the color's great, especially in Act Two. The sound is excellent. Includes curtain call and playbill scans.

Cast: Emily Barber (Ewen Montagu & Others), Seán Carey (Charles Cholmondeley & Others), Chlöe Hart (Johnny Bevan & Others), Christian Andrews (Hester Leggatt & Others), Claire-Marie Hall (Jean Leslie & Others)
Master Notes: Solid capture of one of my favorite shows currently running in the world. Shot in a fantastic mix of wides, mediums, and close-ups. Some increased wandering, shaking, and re-adjusting throughout. Act 1 is impeccable, some of my best work, with only a bar in the way at the bottom that doesn't block anything. Act 2 is more mixed, especially in the first 20-ish minutes, but still captures a lot of action with only a few dropouts throughout and some obstruction and wandering/shaking that goes away afterwards. Some rustling can be heard throughout. Overall a very good capture.
Trading Notes: Not Applicable

Cast: David Bedella (Dr. Frank-N-Furter), Haley Flaherty (Janet Weiss), Ben Forster (Brad Majors), Jayde Westaby (Magenta/Usherette), Sophie Linder-Lee (Columbia), Kristian Lavercombe (Riff Raff), Richard Meek (Eddie/Dr. Scott), Dominic Andersen (Rocky), Richard O'Brien (The Narrator), Stephen Fry (g/p The Narrator), Emma Bunton (g/p The Narrator), Adrian Edmondson (g/p The Narrator), Anthony Stewart Head (g/p The Narrator)
Trading Notes:
Proshot. Includes special guests as Narrators - Richard OBrien, Stephen Fry, Anthony Head, Adrian Edmondson, Emma Bunton, and Mel Giedroyc. Two versions exist:

BBC America Broadcast's version is censored for American audiences, which silences out many of the great audience callbacks and 4th wall breaks. The UK Theatrical Live Cinema Screening is the uncensored version, which includes interviews with audience members and cast/crew before the show and during intermission.

The subtitles provided only sync to the uncensored version screened in cinemas, not to the cut-down TV rips.

A hybrid version also exists, which combines the uncensored audio of the UK version with the superior video quality of the American version.

Master Notes: A fantastic capture. This was filmed in the stalls and is a rather painful theatre to film in due to the poor rake on all levels. However, this recording only has one head in frame throughout and it doesn’t block any of the actors or action. A few blackouts due to the people next to me leaving to go to the bathroom and then leaving early in the second act. The audio does contain some rustling as the child next to me was eating sweets or popcorn. It’s annoying but not the worst. Filmed in wides and mediums. Recording includes the curtain call and marquee.

Cast: Andrew Scott (Performer)
Trading Notes:
Andrew Scott (Fleabag) brings multiple characters to life in Simon Stephens’ (The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time) radical new version of Chekhov’s Uncle Vanya.

Hopes, dreams, and regrets are thrust into sharp focus in this one-man adaptation which explores the complexities of human emotions.

Filmed live during its sold-out run in London’s West End. Directed by Sam Yates and designed by Rosanna Vize.

Cast: Laurel Harris (s/b Elphaba), Katie Rose Clarke (Glinda), Ryan McCartan (Fiyero), Michael McCormick (The Wizard), Nancy Opel (Madame Morrible), Gizel Jimenez (Nessarose), Jesse JP Johnson (Boq), Jamie Jackson (Doctor Dillamond), Manuel Herrera (Chistery), Kathy Santen (Midwife), Sterling Masters (Witch's Mother), William Ryall (Witch's Father/Ozian Official)
Trading Notes: This is a vivid 4K video with almost no obstructions. Pretty much everything is centered, with beautiful zooms but also wider shots that showcase the dancing and set design. At times the video appears on an angle because my wrists are weak, but you don't miss anything because of it. There is also washout at times, but I do believe my camera handles the spotlights better than in a lot of Wicked videos. Act 2 starts a minute into the performance. All in all, this is a gorgeous capture of old and new cast members... I'm not finished yet in this bootleg business but if I was, this could be my magnum opus.

Cast: Idina Menzel (Elphaba), Kristin Chenoweth (Glinda), Norbert Leo Butz (Fiyero), Joel Grey (The Wizard), Carole Shelley (Madame Morrible), Michelle Federer (Nessarose), Christopher Fitzgerald (Boq), William Youmans (Doctor Dillamond)
Trading Notes:
Shot from the rear mezz with no heads in the way. An amazing quality capture and great vocals from the cast. A.
Multiple copies circulate:
VOB + Smalls (3.65 GB) - 1st generation from the master. 1 Disc. Unfortunately, the actual tape transfer is rather bad and results in a lot of washout. Includes extras: NY1 Review Segment, Today Show performance and Letterman performance.
VOB + Smalls (4.80 GB) & (7.46 GB) - 2 Disc copy. Noted as the better version of this video. Unfortunately, the color is semi off and the picture is rather soft.
VOB + Smalls (2.38 GB) - 1st generation master tape re-transferred by NYCG8R in 2011. 1 Disc. Amazingly clearer picture and more vibrant colors. Includes an outside shot of a Wicked poster before the show starts. Screenshots below are from this version.
Release Format: {2011 Master Tape Re-Transfer} - VOB + Smalls (2.38 GB)

Cast: Idina Menzel (Elphaba), Kristin Chenoweth (Glinda), Norbert Leo Butz (Fiyero), Sean McCourt (u/s The Wizard), Carole Shelley (Madame Morrible), Michelle Federer (Nessarose), Randy Harrison (t/r Boq), William Youmans (Doctor Dillamond)
Trading Notes:
This is Kristin and Norbert's last show. It was also supposed to be Joel Grey's last show, but he was ill. Video is sound only for the first 4 minutes and for the last 30 seconds of Defying Gravity. This is a great video with excellent sound. Includes the speech by Idina. This bootleg is a treasure.
Multiple copies are circulating. The best is the 7.8 GB file, which has higher video resolution, working menus, and the playbill. The 7.63 GB file has the same video quality but no playbill, skips 1 minute in late Act 1 (A Sentimental Man (Reprise)), skips the first 1.5 minutes of Act 2, and the smalls may glitch such that the last section of Act 1 doesn't play at all. The 4.07 GB file has worse video quality but does have a working menu, and includes the playbill and various extras (TV spots, Macy's special, Seth's Broadway Chatterbox).
